title: VoiceMode daily-driver release
description: Deliver the approved four-pillar VoiceMode daily-driver design: deterministic conversation kernel, explicit Codex attachment, reliable realtime audio interaction, and self-healing operations. Definition of done is in docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.

title: DD-001 Pin broker daily-driver behavioral baselines
description: Capture deterministic protocol-v1, Codex JSONL, wake/follow-up, acknowledgment, one-final, one-TTS, and child-voice-disable fixtures in tests/fixtures/broker and existing broker tests. Acceptance: no production behavior change; fixtures contain no secrets and repeat byte-identically. Verify focused broker tests and git diff --check. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.
close_reason: Pinned protocol, Codex-response, cue, and privacy-safe golden baselines; broker suite passes 123 tests.

title: DD-023 Implement app-server turns steering interruption and approvals
description: Map request_id to client user-message ID; implement turn start, capability-gated steer, interrupt, completion dedupe, and visual approval forwarding. Preserve Codex sandbox/approval ownership. Acceptance: shared conformance covers start/steer/reject/interrupt/approval/out-of-order/duplicate events. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.
close_reason: Implemented native app-server start/steer/interrupt, bounded event correlation, canonical completion deduplication, safe visual approvals, hands-free native adapter integration, exact thread preservation, and 321 passing broker tests.

title: DD-024 Recover Codex host connection without duplicate work
description: Freeze dispatch on disconnect, reconnect with bounded backoff, reattach explicit thread, query request disposition, and classify completed/absent/uncertain. Acceptance: fault injection around dispatch/completion never redispatches or re-presents and always records rationale. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.
close_reason: Implemented bounded app-server reconnect with dispatch freeze, exact thread reattachment, durable request correlation, completed/absent/in-progress/uncertain decisions, journaled rationale, and circuit breaking. Full broker suite: 364 passed.

title: DD-025 Bring exec Codex fallback under host contract
description: Wrap current codex exec adapter, advertise only supported capabilities, keep schema-constrained response and child MCP disablement, terminate process group on cancellation, and label separate thread. Acceptance: conformance passes supported methods and unsupported actions fail before dispatch. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.
close_reason: Implemented honest exec-host capabilities, correlated async completion, process-group cancellation, and verified 371 broker tests plus Ruff.

title: DD-026 Integrate host adapters into hands-free loop
description: Replace concrete Codex child factory with host selection, display adapter/thread/repo before readiness, and route claim-journal-host-presenter asynchronously. Acceptance: app-server voice turns retain context; exec fallback cannot silently appear as current session. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.
close_reason: Integrated lifecycle-managed app-server and exec host adapters into hands-free dispatch, durable journaling, recovery, canonical presentation, explicit startup identity, remote-stop interruption, and 1.25x speech. Full broker suite: 372 passed; Ruff clean.

title: DD-030 Extract hybrid end-of-speech detector
description: Create pure endpoint detector with adaptive noise floor, VAD voting, mostly-silent tail, min/max duration, PTT release, and optional secondary linguistic evidence. Acceptance: noise/pause/clipping fixtures meet endpoint budget without known truncation. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.
close_reason: Implemented hybrid endpoint detector with adaptive noise floor, VAD voting, bounded silent tails, endpoint reasons, capture integration, and 387 broker tests passing

title: DD-031 Add cancellation-aware audio session and device recovery
description: Create AudioSession owning persistent input/output, cancellable single playback, serialized device reopen, and final close. Acceptance: cancel/device-loss races leak no streams, replay no audio, and never create a second owner. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.
close_reason: Added single-owner AudioSession, cancellable buffered output, serialized bounded device recovery, race-safe cleanup, 21 focused tests, and 396 broker tests passing

title: DD-032 Add activation hotkey and push-to-talk adapters
description: Define wake/PTT/toggle/sleep/interrupt events, keep local STT wake fallback, add optional platform hotkey plus terminal fallback, and use PTT release as exact endpoint. Acceptance: conflicts explain exact config; failure leaves another deterministic activation path. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.
close_reason: Added pure activation reducer, optional global push-to-talk, foreground terminal fallback, exact release endpointing, wake bypass, actionable conflict output, and 407 broker tests passing

title: DD-033 Implement capability-aware interruption and barge-in
description: Cancel playback immediately on hotkey, enable acoustic barge-in only when topology/echo checks qualify, route host interrupt/steer by capability, and resolve completion races by request ID. Acceptance: no old audio resumes or redirected utterance dispatches twice. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.
close_reason: done

title: DD-034 Formalize cue and transcript presentation policy
description: Map one rising/falling/interruption/failure cue to named events with dedupe; keep idle/blank/ambient/timeouts silent; render one TTY state line and deterministic non-TTY JSONL. Acceptance: event goldens contain exact cues and no overlaps or duplicates. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.
close_reason: done

title: DD-035 Benchmark and qualify audio hardware behavior
description: Add privacy-safe latency benchmark, manual built-in/USB/wired/Bluetooth/noise/device-rotation matrix, and 100-turn synthetic soak. Acceptance: reference setup meets budgets; unsupported acoustic barge-in clearly falls back to hotkey. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.
notes: Implementation committed as 3992337; automated qualifier, budget failure semantics, docs, and 100-turn single-owner soak pass. Keep open until a warmed real-hardware JSON report proves the reference macOS budgets and topology fallback.

title: DD-010 Add deterministic turn envelopes and reducer
description: Implement frozen turn/response/presentation types and a pure reducer in voice_mode/broker/types.py, state.py, and new turns.py. Preserve protocol-v1 phase projection. Acceptance: every state/event pair tested; no sequence emits two dispatch or presentation intents. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.
close_reason: Added frozen turn, response, and presentation contracts plus exhaustive pure reducer tests; 203 focused and 265 full broker tests pass.

title: DD-040 Add supervised broker lifecycle
description: Reuse service rendering to add launchd/systemd broker templates and idempotent install/start/stop/restart/status while retaining foreground run. Acceptance: golden service tests pass; repeated start yields one broker; stop deletes no user data. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.
close_reason: done

title: DD-041 Add intuitive top-level lifecycle CLI
description: Add voicemode start/stop/restart, retain broker aliases, teach obsolete/mistyped commands, document exit categories, and honor non-TTY/NO_COLOR/CI/stdout discipline. Acceptance: first guessed commands work or return one copyable correction. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.
close_reason: Implemented in 983473b: canonical start/stop/restart commands, stable JSON and exit categories, broker help guidance, typo corrections, and 448 passing broker tests.

title: DD-042 Add startup compatibility handshake
description: Probe package/CLI/plugin protocol, providers, and Codex host capabilities before readiness audio; classify blockers/degradation/advisories by capabilities rather than versions alone. Acceptance: stale install/plugin, provider outage, and missing app-server yield deterministic fallback/block commands. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.
notes: Started after DD-041. Implementing capability-based startup compatibility before readiness audio.
close_reason: Implemented in d929eca: pre-audio capability handshake across installed CLI commands/version, broker/plugin protocols, refreshed provider health, and live Codex methods; deterministic block/fallback commands, redacted status projection, and 454 passing broker tests. Live configured-service probe reported supported.

title: DD-043 Expose diagnostic status and capabilities JSON
description: Project runtime/journal into deterministic status and capabilities schemas covering supervisor, audio, providers, adapter, thread, request, latency, commands, env, and exit codes with redaction. Acceptance: pinned JSON is byte-stable and pipes cleanly to jq. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.
notes: Started after DD-042. Building redacted deterministic broker diagnostic projections and machine-readable capabilities.
close_reason: Implemented in 5e91432: deterministic redacted broker status and capabilities JSON, v2 client compatibility, root status integration, SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH support, and 466 passing broker tests.

title: DD-044 Add doctor and explain-last CLI
description: Implement doctor checks for ownership/supervisor/socket/audio/providers/compatibility/Codex/journal and explain-last turn reconstruction. Each failure names evidence and one remediation. Acceptance: fixture failures map to expected commands; healthy output is concise. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.
notes: Started after DD-043. Adding actionable doctor and explain-last commands over the diagnostic projections.

title: DD-045 Coordinate subsystem recovery and circuit breaking
description: Centralize retry budgets and circuit state for audio/STT/TTS/Codex/journal/socket, preserving dispatch disposition and stopping recurring cues. Acceptance: fault matrix converges without unbounded retry, duplicate dispatch, or stale lock. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.

title: DD-046 Update install migration docs and changelog
description: Document canonical lifecycle, app-server attachment vs labeled exec fallback, cues/PTT/interruption/privacy/diagnostics; add idempotent supervised migration and installed-command verification. Acceptance: fresh install and upgrade reach healthy doctor and docs match installed CLI. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.

title: DD-050 Certify daily-driver conformance faults and endurance
description: Add cross-package integration, fault/race tests, 100-turn soak, determinism/stdout/non-TTY gates, and converse/control/service regressions. Acceptance: zero duplicate dispatch/final/TTS, leaked stream, stuck phase, or growing queue. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.

title: DD-051 Run live qualification and default-on decision
description: Install like a user; prove real app-server context, labeled exec fallback, wake/PTT/endpoint/follow-up/approval/interruption/device/provider/broker recovery and eight-hour soak. Keep opt-in on any failed invariant/budget. Acceptance: all design gates pass on real hardware. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.

title: DD-011 Add privacy-safe append-only turn journal
description: Add voice_mode/broker/journal.py with schema-versioned JSONL, atomic append, torn-tail recovery, bounded retention, and transcript-free defaults. Acceptance: crash/corruption tests recover complete records and default records contain no transcript/audio. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.
close_reason: Added schema-versioned privacy-safe JSONL journal with atomic fsync append, private permissions, torn-tail recovery, strict corruption checks, restart-safe sequencing, coalesced bounded retention, injected clocks/writer/scheduler, and 334 passing broker tests.

title: DD-012 Make broker runtime dispatch idempotent
description: Refactor runtime to accept turns and atomically claim/confirm dispatch by request_id, persisting claims before host calls. Acceptance: concurrent claimers yield one dispatch; every crash boundary yields zero automatic redispatch. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.
close_reason: Implemented durable accept/claim/confirm dispatch with atomic exactly-once authorization, conservative restart classification, and persistence-boundary fault coverage; 346 broker tests pass.

title: DD-013 Centralize canonical response presentation
description: Add Presenter and route all final terminal/TTS output through request-ID dedupe. One immutable CanonicalResponse supplies display and speech. Acceptance: duplicate completion, retry, cancellation, and TTS failure produce one visible final and at most one playback. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.
close_reason: Added request-aware canonical Presenter; visible and TTS claims are journaled before side effects, conflicting spoken output is rejected, and retries/failures/cancellation remain at-most-once. Full broker suite: 354 passed.

title: DD-014 Version broker protocol and expose turn identity
description: Add protocol-v2 negotiation and detailed request/adapter/thread/status while preserving exact protocol-v1 fixtures. Acceptance: v1 remains compatible; v2 schemas are pinned; unsupported versions return a stable error and exact correction. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.
close_reason: Added protocol v2 negotiation, exact turn identity and recovery diagnostics, named interrupt, recovery commands, v1 byte compatibility, and golden fixtures. Full broker suite: 379 passed; Ruff clean.

title: DD-020 Define Codex host-adapter contract
description: Create broker/hosts base capability, thread, turn, completion, approval, and error contracts plus a shared conformance harness. Every dispatch accepts request_id. Acceptance: fake adapter exercises every capability/failure and kernel imports only the base contract. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.
close_reason: Defined host-independent capability, thread, turn, completion, approval, disposition, event, and error contracts with reusable fake-adapter conformance; 277 broker tests pass.

title: DD-021 Build bounded Codex app-server JSON-RPC transport
description: Implement initialize, correlated JSON-RPC, notification routing, deadlines, cancellation, message bounds, stderr separation, and protocol-fault closure over stdio/user Unix socket. Acceptance: fragmented, delayed, malformed, cancelled, dead, and restarted transport tests are deterministic. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.
close_reason: Added bounded Codex-native app-server stdio/Unix transport with strict correlation, cancellation, diagnostics, protocol fault handling, and real local initialize handshake; 292 broker tests pass.

title: DD-022 Discover Codex capabilities and select thread deterministically
description: Implement app-server capability probing and selection order: explicit thread, host registration, one matching active repo thread, otherwise clearly labeled new broker thread. Never guess among many. Acceptance: zero/one/many tests and exact adapter/thread visible before capture. Full implementation details and exact files: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-19-voice-daily-driver-plan.md.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic app-server thread discovery and selection, inherited exact CODEX_THREAD_ID support, CLI adapter/thread controls, visible pre-dispatch identity, and 312 passing broker tests.

title: GPT-Realtime Codex operator
description: Implement the approved cloud-first GPT-Realtime operator with a loopback WebRTC client, server-side OpenAI sideband, one asynchronous Codex app-server job, deterministic response arbitration, visible transcript/state, privacy-safe recovery, and one shared microphone owner. Source plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-20-gpt-realtime-codex-operator-plan.md

title: RTOP-001 Capture baseline and external contract fixtures
description:
Purpose:
Freeze current classic behavior and the provider wire assumptions
before new orchestration exists. Characterization tests keep the additive lane
from silently changing commands that users already rely on.

Files:
- `tests/fixtures/realtime/` (new)
- `tests/fixtures/realtime/README.md` (new)
- `tests/test_broker_realtime_contract_fixtures.py` (new)
- existing classic tests are executed but not edited

Acceptance:
No production code changes; fixtures contain only synthetic
content; `test_broker_cli.py`, `test_broker_socket.py`, and
`test_broker_compatibility.py` retain their baseline result.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-20-gpt-realtime-codex-operator-plan.md
notes: Implementing synthetic OpenAI/app-server fixtures and classic broker characterization baseline from the reviewed plan.
close_reason: Synthetic OpenAI Realtime and Codex app-server contract fixtures pinned; scrubber and deterministic serialization checks pass (20 tests), and the classic broker baseline passed twice at 32 tests.

title: RTOP-032 Manage session readiness, reconnect, and 60-minute rollover
description:
Purpose:
Turn low-level transport events into a stable operator session that
survives transient control loss and replaces expiring calls without touching the
Codex job lifecycle.

Files:
- `voice_mode/broker/realtime/session.py` (new)
- `tests/test_broker_realtime_session.py` (new)

Acceptance:
A 61-minute fake-clock session rolls calls without duplicating a
user response, tool effect, job, or worker integration; losing sideband never
leaves browser audio governed only by client JavaScript.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-20-gpt-realtime-codex-operator-plan.md

title: RTOP-040 Build the authenticated loopback server
description:
Purpose:
Expose the minimum browser bootstrap and control surface while
defending against unrelated local pages, DNS rebinding assumptions, oversized
payloads, and accidental repository serving.

Files:
- `voice_mode/broker/realtime/security.py`
- `voice_mode/broker/realtime/web.py` (new)
- `tests/test_broker_realtime_server.py` (new)

Acceptance:
The browser can create and control one authenticated local
session; a page from another origin cannot start audio, post SDP, read the event
stream, or invoke a job control even though both run on the same machine.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-20-gpt-realtime-codex-operator-plan.md
notes: Implementing the authenticated loopback HTTP boundary with strict origin, authority, capability, and payload controls.
close_reason: Authenticated loopback server landed with strict host-origin-bearer checks, closed control payloads, timed controller expiry, hardened error headers, and 9 focused tests plus neighboring realtime suites green.

title: RTOP-041 Build the reference WebRTC transcript and status UI
description:
Purpose:
Make microphone, submission, operator speech, Codex job location,
and failure state visible while using the browser's native WebRTC media stack.

Files:
- `voice_mode/templates/realtime/index.html` (new)
- `voice_mode/templates/realtime/app.js` (new)
- `voice_mode/templates/realtime/styles.css` (new)
- `tests/test_broker_realtime_assets.py` (new)

No additional JavaScript test file or Node dependency is added in this slice;
browser behavior is covered by static contract checks here and the exact manual
runner owned by RTOP-061.

Acceptance:
A user can always see whether cloud audio is off/on, what final
caption was heard, which Codex job/thread is active, whether output is speaking,
and whether a control succeeded; stopping the page ends its microphone track.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-20-gpt-realtime-codex-operator-plan.md

title: RTOP-052A Add the realtime audio lease and classic compatibility guard
description:
Purpose:
Give the realtime session exclusive capture ownership when cloud
audio is engaged without making an idle browser page block classic local voice
or requiring edits to the current hands-free WIP.

Files:
- `voice_mode/broker/audio_lease.py` (new neutral primitive)
- `tests/test_broker_audio_lease.py` (new)
- `voice_mode/broker/realtime/audio_guard.py` (new)
- `voice_mode/broker/realtime/web.py`
- `tests/test_broker_realtime_audio_lease.py` (new)

Acceptance:
An idle realtime page holds no audio lease; authenticated Start
cannot proceed alongside a detected classic audio broker or another lease
holder; Stop releases only its own lease and only after the injected media
teardown contract reports a safe outcome.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-20-gpt-realtime-codex-operator-plan.md

title: RTOP-052B Adopt the neutral lease in classic hands-free mode
description:
Purpose:
Complete the cross-mode guarantee after the current audio lane is
available for coordinated editing, without delaying the standalone realtime
vertical slice.

Files:
- `voice_mode/broker/handsfree.py`
- `tests/test_broker_handsfree.py`
- `tests/test_broker_audio_lease.py`

No edit to `voice_mode/broker/audio.py` or `tests/test_broker_audio.py` is part
of this task; their post-WIP behavior is run as regression evidence.

Acceptance:
Classic and realtime processes share one neutral capture lease,
neither can steal it, and existing classic controls and local-only operation
remain unchanged.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-20-gpt-realtime-codex-operator-plan.md

title: RTOP-050 Compose the operator runtime and bounded tool dispatcher
description:
Purpose:
Join the independently tested planes in one composition root with
deterministic startup, ownership, and shutdown instead of cross-importing
components until they happen to run.

Files:
- `voice_mode/broker/realtime/runner.py` (new)
- `voice_mode/broker/realtime/status.py` (new)
- `voice_mode/broker/realtime/__init__.py`
- `tests/test_broker_realtime_runner.py` (new)
- `tests/test_broker_realtime_integration.py` (new)

Acceptance:
One runner can execute the full fake vertical slice with one
host dispatch, concurrent voice events, one worker delivery, one response per
cause, and no resource leak or dirty classic import.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-20-gpt-realtime-codex-operator-plan.md

title: RTOP-051 Add configuration, CLI, status discovery, and packaging
description:
Purpose:
Make the slice installable and diagnosable through one intuitive
command without changing classic defaults or requiring repository checkout
assets.

Files:
- `voice_mode/config.py`
- `voice_mode/cli_commands/broker.py`
- `voice_mode/broker/diagnostics.py`
- `tests/test_broker_realtime_cli.py` (new)
- `tests/test_broker_realtime_assets.py`
- `tests/test_broker_cli.py`

The existing Hatch template include already covers these assets, so no
`pyproject.toml` edit is expected; the wheel test verifies that assumption.

Acceptance:
A built wheel exposes `voicemode broker realtime`, serves all
assets, starts with zero live API calls before browser Start, and leaves every
classic command/output contract compatible.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-20-gpt-realtime-codex-operator-plan.md

title: RTOP-060 Add cross-plane conformance and fault integration
description:
Purpose:
Prove the operator, worker, transport, browser boundary, journal,
and classic broker remain correct under duplicate, reordered, disconnected, and
crashing event sequences.

Files:
- `tests/fakes/realtime.py`
- `tests/test_broker_realtime_integration.py`
- `tests/test_broker_realtime_faults.py` (new)
- `tests/test_broker_realtime_soak.py` (new)
- `tests/test_broker_realtime_security.py` (new)
- `tests/fixtures/realtime/fault-cases.json` (new)

Acceptance:
The harness records zero duplicate host mutations, responses,
function outputs, worker deliveries, or visible finals; zero worker-caused tool
effects; zero leaked tasks, sessions, peers under the fake boundary, streams, or
lease descriptors; no audio-eligible peer after policy/liveness loss; bounded
memory/queues; and an unchanged classic test surface.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-20-gpt-realtime-codex-operator-plan.md

title: RTOP-061 Run explicit real-service and microphone qualification
description:
Purpose:
Validate the behavior that fakes cannot establish: actual SDP and
sideband schema compatibility, browser echo handling, semantic endpointing,
barge-in, caption quality, and app-server interaction.

Files:
- `tests/manual/test_broker_realtime.py` (new manual runner/checklist)
- `docs/benchmarks/realtime-operator-baseline.md` (new, privacy-safe results)
- no recorded user audio or transcript fixtures

Acceptance:
The live run completes natural pauses, barge-in, asynchronous
delegation, continued conversation, steering, explicit approval boundary,
single result integration, reconnect, and teardown. Any failed invariant keeps
the mode experimental and blocks RTOP-071.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-20-gpt-realtime-codex-operator-plan.md

title: RTOP-070 Document operation, privacy, troubleshooting, and changelog
description:
Purpose:
Make the opt-in mode understandable from installed help and user
documentation, with cloud/audio boundaries and exact recovery steps visible
before release.

Files:
- `docs/reference/broker.md`
- `docs/reference/cli.md`
- `docs/guides/configuration.md`
- `docs/concepts/architecture.md`
- `docs/guides/realtime-codex-operator.md` (new)
- `README.md`
- `CHANGELOG.md` under `## [Unreleased]`

Acceptance:
A user can install the wheel, understand that audio is cloud,
start the mode, find the Codex task, interpret every UI state, stop it, and
resolve common failures without reading source or raw logs.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-20-gpt-realtime-codex-operator-plan.md

title: RTOP-010 Add provider-neutral realtime types and limits
description:
Purpose:
Give the operator plane a closed vocabulary before network or host
code can accidentally couple remote dictionaries to runtime state.

Files:
- `voice_mode/broker/realtime/__init__.py` (new)
- `voice_mode/broker/realtime/types.py` (new)
- `voice_mode/broker/realtime/security.py` (new, validation constants only)
- `tests/test_broker_realtime_types.py` (new)

Acceptance:
Types have no aiohttp, OpenAI, browser, or concrete host import;
all public JSON is deterministic and secret-free.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-20-gpt-realtime-codex-operator-plan.md
notes: Implementing provider-neutral realtime types, strict limits, and loopback security primitives from the reviewed plan.
close_reason: Added provider-neutral realtime IDs, states, immutable public values, limits, canonical JSON, strict loopback/capability validation, repository-root allowlisting, and bounded secret redaction; 60 focused tests and ruff pass.

title: RTOP-071 Make the opt-in release decision
description:
Purpose:
Ship only after code, package, docs, privacy boundaries, and real
interaction satisfy the approved design; otherwise preserve a useful testable
experimental lane without overstating readiness.

Files:
- `docs/benchmarks/realtime-operator-baseline.md`
- `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-20-gpt-realtime-codex-operator-plan.md`

No production or release-artifact edit is part of this task. A separate
explicit release request owns the existing release workflow.

Acceptance:
All automated and manual invariants pass, no secret/privacy
finding remains, package/docs match, and outstanding limitations are explicit.
If not, close the completed engineering beads but leave this gate open with the
specific evidence needed.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-20-gpt-realtime-codex-operator-plan.md

title: RTOP-011 Extend the journal and build realtime replay
description:
Purpose:
Make duplicate prevention and recovery durable with the existing
privacy model instead of adding a second weaker log format.

Files:
- `voice_mode/broker/journal.py`
- `voice_mode/broker/realtime/journal.py` (new)
- `tests/test_broker_journal.py`
- `tests/test_broker_realtime_journal.py` (new)

Acceptance:
A fresh manager can decide which effects are safe, complete, or
uncertain using only journal records; classic journal tests remain green.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-20-gpt-realtime-codex-operator-plan.md
notes: Implementing durable realtime journal extension and pure replay with shared-writer hardening.

title: RTOP-012 Implement the single-response arbiter
description:
Purpose:
Put “one response” in a pure deterministic reducer rather than
relying on Realtime defaults, browser timing, or prompt obedience.

Files:
- `voice_mode/broker/realtime/arbiter.py` (new)
- `tests/test_broker_realtime_arbiter.py` (new)

Acceptance:
No legal or duplicate event sequence emits two response-create
actions for one cause or two worker responses for one delivery ID, and a worker
cause can never emit a tool-authorizing action.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-20-gpt-realtime-codex-operator-plan.md

title: RTOP-013 Promote request recovery to the host contract
description:
Purpose:
Give every job-recovery caller one typed host boundary instead of
requiring app-server downcasts, optional-method checks, or knowledge of thread
history payloads.

Files:
- `voice_mode/broker/hosts/base.py`
- `voice_mode/broker/hosts/app_server.py`
- `tests/test_broker_host_contract.py`
- `tests/test_broker_app_server.py`

Acceptance:
RTOP-021 can call `adapter.recover_request(...)` through a
`HostAdapter` reference for every supported adapter, and recovery never depends
on a private concrete seam.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-20-gpt-realtime-codex-operator-plan.md
notes: Promoting request recovery into the HostAdapter contract with direct app-server and unsupported-adapter coverage.
close_reason: Promoted recover_request into the HostAdapter contract with typed default evidence and app-server history-backed recovery coverage; focused recovery lane passed and full host adapter files passed at 35 tests.

title: RTOP-020 Implement the one-job asynchronous manager
description:
Purpose:
Separate host turn acceptance from host completion so the voice
session remains available while Codex works, without altering the blocking
hands-free runner.

Files:
- `voice_mode/broker/realtime/jobs.py` (new)
- `tests/fakes/__init__.py` (new)
- `tests/fakes/realtime.py` (new)
- `tests/test_broker_realtime_jobs.py` (new)

Acceptance:
A long fake Codex turn can remain running while ten independent
operator events are processed; exactly one host `start_turn` and one worker
terminal event occur.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-20-gpt-realtime-codex-operator-plan.md

title: RTOP-021 Add host recovery, approval visibility, and job retention
description:
Purpose:
Preserve existing evidence-based dispatch semantics when the
app-server connection fails, and expose approval blocking without creating a
voice authorization path.

Files:
- `voice_mode/broker/realtime/jobs.py`
- `voice_mode/broker/realtime/journal.py`
- `tests/test_broker_realtime_job_recovery.py` (new)

No edit to `voice_mode/broker/recovery.py` or `tests/test_broker_recovery.py` is
part of this task; the existing recovery suite is run as regression evidence.

Acceptance:
No recovery path redispatches an uncertain host request;
approvals are visible and actionable in Codex but impossible to grant through
the four Realtime tools.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-20-gpt-realtime-codex-operator-plan.md

title: RTOP-030 Build pure OpenAI event validation and client-event builders
description:
Purpose:
Keep a changing external schema at one strict boundary so
orchestration code consumes stable semantic events and tests can pin current
official behavior.

Files:
- `voice_mode/broker/realtime/protocol.py` (new)
- `tests/test_broker_realtime_protocol.py` (new)
- `tests/fixtures/realtime/openai-*.json` (from RTOP-001)

Acceptance:
All supported external dictionaries become typed semantic
events before reaching session or job code; no unsupported tool name can be
executed.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-20-gpt-realtime-codex-operator-plan.md
notes: Implementing pure OpenAI Realtime event validation and deterministic client-event builders against pinned fixtures.
close_reason: Pure OpenAI Realtime event validation and deterministic client-event builders landed with 12 protocol tests passing and ruff clean.

title: RTOP-031 Implement multipart SDP and sideband WebSocket transport
description:
Purpose:
Provide one injectable OpenAI I/O adapter with deterministic
timeouts, cleanup, limits, and redaction while keeping policy in higher layers.

Files:
- `voice_mode/broker/realtime/transport.py` (new)
- `tests/test_broker_realtime_transport.py` (new)

Acceptance:
A scripted transport proves call creation and sideband event
flow without live network, and secret scanning of logs/errors/status finds no
credential, call ID, or SDP.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-20-gpt-realtime-codex-operator-plan.md
